observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

Draw the necessary adjusting journal entries for the noted errors after verification and supported with documents;



Reconcile the reciprocal accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds, and the balances between the ledgers, journals, and trial balance; and



Thoroughly review the accounts and observe the proper recording and presentation of transactions in the books of accounts and the financial statements.
observations.reason:
Adjusting entries has already been made for audit findings no. 2, however, no adjustments has been made for audit finding no. 1 and 3.






The unreconciled difference of Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds has been reduced, however, a difference between the two accounts still remain.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management cause the reversion of the ₱775,545.13 LDRRMF unutilized balance for more than five years from the TL-DRRMF account to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund, to be made available for other social services after subsequent enactment by the local sanggunian.  

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

Strictly monitor the unexpended DRRMF by maintaining subsidiary ledgers by year of transfer and initiate its reversion to the General Fund books after the lapse of the five-year validity period;

Draw the necessary adjustments in the General Fund and Trust Fund books, relative to the errors noted; and

Strictly adhere to the LDRRMF accounting guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.reason:
Reiterated in this report.










Subsidiary Ledgers by year of transfer is not maintained for the unexpended DRRMF




There were still some noted non-compliance with COA Circular 2012-002

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare a duly certified list showing the taxpayers and amounts due and collectible and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year, to serve as the basis for setting up the RPT and SET Receivables in the books.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer did not furnish her a list showing the taxpayers and amounts due them.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant/officials concerned submit the distribution lists and other lacking documents, pertaining to the above-mentioned transactions, and henceforth, thoroughly check the completeness of supporting documents before processing claims, to avoid suspensions in audit.
observations.reason: The Audit Team has not received the distribution lists being required to support the transactions made.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the JO personnel submit approved DTRs, supported with authenticated copies of the logbook/biometric printouts, and Accomplishment Reports certified correct by the officials who have direct supervision over their work, specifying in detail the work accomplishments for the particular period;

The Municipal Accountant ensures the completeness of supporting documents before claims are processed.
observations.reason:
The Report of Disbursements for wages of JO personnel did not include authenticated copies of the logbook/biometric printouts.




Authenticated copies of logbook / biometric printouts were still lacking.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct accountable officers/officials concerned, to promptly prepare their deliverables, and submit them to/furnish the Municipal Accountant, to facilitate the timely recording of transactions and the preparation of the financial statements.

Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit the financial statements to COA within the prescribed period, with complete supporting schedules and documents.
observations.reason: Although the submission of the FS for CY 2024 was still delayed, the noted delay was less compared to previous years submission

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the: 

Acting Municipal Accountant to prepare monthly bank reconciliation statements for all five (5) bank accounts supported with bank statements and schedules, and further to record all the reconciling items/adjustments to the accounts accordingly, and submit subsidiary and general ledgers for CIB-LCCA for all funds to substantiate the reported account in the consolidated financial statements.

Municipal Treasurer to have a regular reconciliation with the records of the Acting Municipal Accountant, monitor cancelled, outstanding, unreleased and stale checks, in the recording of collection and deposits and disbursements and other reconciling items so that appropriate adjustments could be made on a monthly basis.
observations.reason:
Reiterated in this report






Regular reconciliation of records between the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer was not undertaken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that a Lapsing Schedule for Depreciation be prepared to serve as a guide in the computation of the annual depreciation.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant failed to prepare lapsing schedule for depreciation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:

Acting Municipal Accountant to: 

Coordinate with the designated property officer to reconstruct and reconcile their records, taking into consideration the accuracy of the beginning inventory balances, the purchases during the year that were recorded as inventory, the purchases during the year that were reclassified to expenses, the actual issuances, and other related adjustments, and prepare the necessary adjusting entries; and

Coordinate with the Inventory Committee and the designated property officer to obtain the balance of inventory per physical count at the end of the year, reconcile balances and prepare adjusting entries, if warranted.

Municipal Treasurer to: 

Prepare regularly the RIS and SSMI and furnish the Acting Municipal Accountant a copy thereof for recording; and 

Reconcile property records with the result of annual physical count and accounting records.

Inventory Committee to: 

Submit CY 2021 RPCI; and 

Regularly conduct physical count of inventories and submit a copy of the RPCI to the Audit Team not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semester, respectively.
observations.reason:
Reconciliation of records was not undertaken.






Inventory of supplies was not conducted






The GSO failed to prepare RIS and SSMI.






The RPCI was not prepared, thus, reconciliation was not undertaken.






RPCI was not prepared.






Inventory of supplies was not conducted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) undertake measures that would address the causes cited by the Acting Municipal Accountant, if warranted, in order to submit the FS on the date prescribed by the pertinent regulation in force.
observations.reason: Although the submission of the FS for CY 2024 was still delayed, the noted delay was less compared to previous years submission

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to transfer the Trust Liability-DRRMF from the General Fund to the Trust Fund.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to exert effort in tracing the unreconciled balance found in the CIB-LCCA of the General Fund.
observations.reason: There were still no bank reconciliation statements prepared.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal General Service Officer to conduct physical inventory of all remaining items of goods, supplies, materials including drugs and medicines and medical supplies and equipment in the custody of responsible officials and determine undistributed/unconsumed items. Likewise, require the submission of Stock Cards, Requisition Issue Slip, Inventory Custodian Slip, and such other records/reports supporting issuance/distribution of the inventories. 

Further, required the Municipal Accountant to recognize in the books any undistributed/unissued inventory items by preparing a Journal Entry Voucher to record the inventory items in the corresponding inventory ledger card.
observations.reason:
Inventory of goods and supplies was not conducted.






The Municipal Accountant cannot record in the books the undistributed/ unissued inventory items due to non-preparation of RPCI

observations.recommendation: We recommended that a Lapsing Schedule for Depreciation be prepared to serve as a guide in the computation of annual depreciation.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant failed to prepare lapsing schedule for depreciation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the MPDC, Municipal Assessor, Municipal Engineer, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to coordinate in identification, inventory, measurement, recording and maintenance of records and reports for local roads and road components under the jurisdiction of the LGU.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant to fully disclose the details of the Local Road Network in the Notes to the Financial Statements and prepare adjustments to the account to reflect corrections for transaction in CY 2019.
observations.reason:
According to the department heads concerned, they were already able to identify some of the road networks, however, the file that they made relative to the inventory of road networks was lost.




Not Implemented | According to the department heads concerned, they were already able to identify some of the road networks, however, the file that they made relative to the inventory of road networks was lost.



Due to incomplete data and failure of the inventory committee to complete physical count of the road networks, the Municipal Accountant failed to disclose in the Notes to the FS the details of the local road networks of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to observe strictly the submission of monthly trial balances and interim financial statements within the period set by regulation.
observations.reason: In CY 2024, the Municipal Accountant did not submit monthly trial balance and quarterly financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer, being the General Service Officer, to conduct and submit Report on the Physical Count on PPE and maintain property cards for each PPE account and the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPLEC), and require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records to come up with a correct balance of PPE.
observations.reason:
The RPCPPE for CY 2024 was not prepared.  Likewise, the GSO failed to prepare property cards.

The Municipal Accountant was only able to prepare PPE ledger cards for CY 2022 and onwards purchased PPEs, and still on the process of preparing one for all PPEs owned by the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to gather all necessary data pertaining to the account and determine appropriate action thereof in accordance with COA Circular 2016-005.
observations.reason: The Audit Team has yet to receive request for write-off of the long outstanding receivable.  In fact, the Municipal Accountant disclosed in the Notes to the FS that they are still in the process of requesting death certificate of the former officer from the PSA Office.

observations.recommendation:
1.7	We recommended that:
The Municipal Accountant coordinates with the Property Officer/Inventory Committee for the reconciliation of records to accurately and reliably present the PPE accounts in the financial statements.
The GSO-Designate/Property Officer –
Compare existing records with the Municipal Accountant for tracing/ identification of PPE to be accounted in the RPCPPE, and reconcile PPE items and their balances between the accounting and property records; and
Prepare and maintain Property Cards.
The Chairperson, Inventory Committee –
Conduct an inventory of all Property, Plant and Equipment annually and submit to the Audit Team on or before January 31 of the ensuing year, and
Communicate to the Municipal Accountant the result of the physical count of PPE to update her records.
1.8	We further reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that management conduct a One-Time Cleansing of PPE Account Balances, following the guidelines and procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, to address the problem of reconciliation of PPE items in the accounting and property records, which have become a perennial issue and caused the non-establishment of the accuracy of the PPE balances presented in the financial statements.

2.9	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the CY 2024 BRS for each bank account maintained by the Municipality, duly supported with documents, and submit the same to COA for verification. Henceforth, strictly observe its monthly preparation and submission to the COA.
2.10	The Municipal Accountant stated that the LGU is now able to electronically generate its bank statements, hence, the Bank Reconciliation Statements can already be easily prepared. She assured compliance with the recommendation in CY 2025.

3.10	We recommended that the General Services Officer submit the lacking requested copies of AIRs and delivery receipts for CY 2024.
3.11	We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant only record obligations as Accounts Payable if they meet the definition; otherwise, record them to a more appropriate liability account.

5.10	We recommended that Management cause the reversion of the ₱1,444,180.33 LDRRMF unutilized balance for five years or more from the Trust Liability-DRRM account to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund, to be made available for other social services after subsequent enactment by the local sanggunian.
5.11	We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Strictly monitor the unexpended DRRMF by maintaining subsidiary ledgers by year of transfer and initiate its reversion to the General Fund books after the lapse of the five-year validity period;
Draw the necessary adjustments in the General Fund and Trust Fund books, relative to the errors noted; and
Strictly adhere to the LDRRMF accounting guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
5.12	The LCE agreed to revert the LDRRMF in the STF, which has been unutilized for five years or more, to the General Fund.
5.13	The Municipal Accountant agreed to comply with the audit recommendations.

6.6	We recommended that the LGU pay the HDMF the identified lacking contributions for CY 2024, totaling ₱24,825.66.
6.7	The officials concerned agreed to comply with the audit recommendation.

We recommended that:

The Municipal Accountant–

Draw the necessary adjustments for the recording of errors noted upon determination of the correct amounts and supported with documents, for the proper valuation of the PPE; and

Coordinate with the Property Officer/Inventory Committee for the reconciliation of records, to accurately and reliably present the PPE accounts in the financial statements.

The GSO-Designate/Property Officer –

Compare existing records with the Municipal Accountant for tracing/ identification of PPE to be accounted in the RPCPPE, and reconcile PPE items and their balances between the accounting and property records; and

Prepare and maintain Property Cards.

We further reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that management conduct a One-Time Cleansing of PPE Account Balances, following the guidelines and procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, to address the problem of reconciliation of PPE items in the accounting and property records, which have become a perennial issue and caused the non-establishment of the accuracy of the PPE balances presented in the financial statements.
observations.reason:
Reiterated in this report






Reiterated in this report






Reiterated in this report






Reiterated in this report

No adjustments have been recorded by the Municipal Accountant








The LGU can now generate bank statements electronically. Hence, preparation of CY 2025 BRSs is expected.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LGU: 

Obtain a certification from the identified beneficiaries, showing the same signatures as presented in their respective valid IDs, to prove that they have indeed received the stipend for the 1st Semester of CY 2023; and

Submit the identified lacking documents to the Auditor and DSWD FO VIII
We also recommended that the SDO and the focal person of the social pension program:

Verify the validity of the signatures on the payroll and that appearing on the presented valid ID; and

Check the completeness of documents before giving the stipend to the beneficiaries or their authorized representatives.

We recommended that the LGU comply with the stipulations in the Memoranda of Agreement executed with the source agencies for all funds received for the implementation of projects/programs/activities, and undertake liquidation thereof within the agreed period.
We recommended that the SDO submit immediately the Liquidation Reports and supporting documents to the ICFAU for the unliquidated cash advances under the Confidential Fund.
observations.reason: The disbursements for Confidential Funds for CY 2022 was reimbursed by the SDO

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The Municipal Accountant –

Trace back details of PPE acquisitions, recompute the accumulated depreciation of the CY 2022 reported balance of depreciable assets taking into account the appropriate residual value for each PPE, and the pertinent provisions in COA Circular No. 2015-008 and PAG3.  Thereafter, effect the necessary adjustments upon determination of the correct amounts, for the proper valuation of the PPE;

Draw adjusting entries for the previously derecognized PPEs, and henceforth, facilitate dropping of PPEs from the books only upon the actual disposal of the assets; and

Maintain complete PPE ledger cards/subsidiary records for items of PPE and coordinate with the Property Officer/Inventory Committee for the identification of recorded assets that were not accounted in the RPCPPE.  Undertake reconciliation of records, to accurately and reliably present the PPE accounts in the financial statements.

The GSO-Designate/Property Officer –

Compare existing records with the Municipal Accountant for tracing/identification of PPE which were not accounted in the RPCPPE, and reconcile PPE items and their balances between the accounting and property records; and

Prepare and maintain complete Property Cards.

Inventory Committee Chairperson include real properties in the physical count of PPE.

We further recommended that management conduct a One-Time Cleansing of PPE Account Balances, following the guidelines and procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, to address the problem of unidentified PPE items in the accounting records and discrepancies in PPE account balances, which have become a perennial issue and caused the non-establishment of the accuracy of the PPE balances presented in the financial statements.
observations.reason:
No adjustments have been made for the CY 2022 errors in depreciation.










The Municipal Accountant does not maintain a complete set of PPE ledger cards.










Reiterated in this report






No Inventory of all PPEs was conducted for CY 2024






Reiterated in this report

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to transfer the duty of releasing checks to the Municipal Treasurer. 

The Municipal Accountant record adjustments for the restoration of unreleased checks at the end of the year to the cash account, to fairly present the balances of the Cash and Payable accounts at year-end.

The Municipal Treasurer – 

Ensure that the release of checks is properly acknowledged by the payees in the Check Register and in the DVs, with dates of receipt indicated therein, for monitoring;

Promptly submit the paid DVs and supporting documents to the Accounting Office to facilitate timely recording of transactions and preparation of financial reports by the Municipal Accountant; and

Prepare a list of unreleased checks at year-end, and furnish the Municipal Accountant, to serve as her basis for drawing adjustments.
observations.reason:
No recorded adjustments for unreleased checks because the amount and number of checks unreleased were not identified.






The Municipal Treasurer is still incurring delay in the submission of DVs to the Municipal Accountant.






List of unreleased checks was not prepared by the Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that that the Municipal Accountant:

Transfer the identified unexpended balances of the QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund;

Reconcile the balance of the Trust Liability-Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund presented in the post-closing trial balance and Note 9 in the Notes to Financial Statements, with that disclosed in Note 27, and make the necessary adjustments, if necessary; and

Revert back to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund the amounts in the Trust Liability-Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund that have been unutilized after five years or more.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report

observations.recommendation: We recommended the submission of the DVs with supporting documents for the CY 2022 transactions charged from the Trust Fund.  This is without prejudice to the issuance of a Notice of Suspension/Disallowance, if warranted.
observations.reason: Check Nos. 926852 and 926853 were not yet submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the following responsible officials: 

Municipal Treasurer to submit regularly the monthly RCDs, monthly/CRAAFs With and Without Money Value for all funds to support the submitted issued and cancelled ORs, reported collections and deposit slips to the Acting Municipal Accountant and to the Audit Team and to monitor the correct ORs requisitioned, issued and balance on hand.

Acting Municipal Accountant to:

Have a regular reconciliation between Collectors’, Municipal Treasurer and the Acting Municipal Accountant’s records in the recording of collections and deposits and other reconciling items so that appropriate adjustments could be made on a monthly basis and to substantiate the reported Cash in Local Treasury account in the financial statement.

Prepare the necessary adjusting entries for any identified reconciling items so that the reported Cash in Local Treasury account balance of ₱286,224.62 at year end will be corrected.
observations.reason: Per discussion in the exit conference, it was agreed that the amount be transferred to the appropriate receivable account.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Head of Procuring Entity to:

Ensure that all POs/contracts entered into by the Municipal Government incorporates definite terms and conditions of the procured items; and

Establish an internal control mechanism that would ensure that all POs/contracts entered into by Management are duly accomplished and include all the basic information as prescribed in COA Circular No. 96-010 to protect the interest of the Municipal Government.

We recommended that the LCE should require the GSO to insure all Insurable Properties of the LGU the soonest possible time to ensure that the LGU will be indemnified in case of losses due to man-made and catastrophic events.
observations.reason: Not mandatory being a 4th class municipality

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
1. The Municipal Accountant–
a. Draw the necessary adjustments for the recording of errors noted
upon determination of the correct amounts and supported with
documents, for the proper valuation of the PPE; and
b. Coordinate with the Property Officer/Inventory Committee for the
reconciliation of records, to accurately and reliably present the PPE
accounts in the financial statements.
2. The GSO-Designate/Property Officer –
a. Compare existing records with the Municipal Accountant for tracing/
identification of PPE to be accounted in the RPCPPE, and reconcile
PPE items and their balances between the accounting and property
records; and
b. Prepare and maintain Property Cards.

We further reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that management
conduct a One-Time Cleansing of PPE Account Balances, following the guidelines
and procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020,
to address the problem of reconciliation of PPE items in the accounting and
property records, which have become a perennial issue and caused the non-
establishment of the accuracy of the PPE balances presented in the financial
statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant –
i. Record adjustments for the accounting errors noted, after verification
and supported with documents;
ii. Refrain from drawing unsupported entries in the journals and ledgers,
and henceforth, ensure that all journal entries and ledger postings have
supporting documents attached;
iii. Assign a staff who would be in charge of securing copies of the bank
statements/snapshots and other related documents, to facilitate the prompt
preparation of the monthly BRS; and
iv. Prepare the CY 2023 BRS for each bank account maintained by the
Municipality, duly supported with documents, and submit the same to COA
for verification. Henceforth, strictly observe its monthly preparation and
submission to COA.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. Draw the necessary adjusting journal entries for the noted errors after
verification and supported with documents;
b. Reconcile the reciprocal accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to
Other Funds, and the balances between the ledgers, journals, and trial
balance; and
c. Thoroughly review the accounts and observe the proper recording and
presentation of transactions in the books of accounts and the financial
statements.

We recommended that Management cause the reversion of the ₱775,545.13
LDRRMF unutilized balance for more than five years from the TL-DRRMF
account to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund, to be made
available for other social services after subsequent enactment by the local
sanggunian.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. Strictly monitor the unexpended DRRMF by maintaining subsidiary ledgers
by year of transfer and initiate its reversion to the General Fund books after
the lapse of the five-year validity period;
b. Draw the necessary adjustments in the General Fund and Trust Fund
books, relative to the errors noted; and
c. Strictly adhere to the LDRRMF accounting guidelines set forth in COA
Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to exert effort in tracing the unreconciled balance found in the CIB-LCCA of the General Fund.
observations.reason: Unreconciled balance is still existing as at end of reporting period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal General Service Officer to conduct physical inventory of all remaining items of goods, supplies, materials including drugs and medicines and medical supplies and equipment in the custody of responsible officials and determine undistributed/unconsumed items. Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant to recognize in the books any undistributed/unissued inventory items by preparing a Journal Entry Voucher to record the inventory items in the corresponding inventory ledger card.
observations.reason: The Inventory Committee failed to submit complete report on physical count of inventories. Documents mentioned in the audit recommendation were not yet prepared by the employee concerned.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that all programs, projects and activities pertaining to the additional disaster funds of the Municipality be incorporated in the LDRRM Plan and Budget. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant disclose all LDRRM funds in the Notes to the Financial Statements, including realigned local funds, additional funds received from the National Government, other LGUs and other agencies.
observations.reason: Disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements is yet to be prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the MPDC, Municipal Assessor, Municipal Engineer, Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to coordinate in identification, inventory, measurement, recording and maintenance of records and reports for local roads and road components under the jurisdiction of the LGU. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant to fully disclose the details of the Local Road Network in the Notes to the Financial Statements and prepare adjustments to the account to reflect corrections for transaction in CY 2019.
observations.reason: Waiting for the reports to be submitted by the Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to observe strictly the submission of monthly trial balances and interim financial statements within the period set by regulation.
observations.reason: Late submission of implemented reports by the Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer, being the General Service Officer, to conduct and submit the Report on the Physical Count on PPE and the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPLEC), and require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records to come up with a correct balance of PPE.
observations.reason: The Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment is yet to be made. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant have not maintained PPLEC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to gather all necessary data pertaining to the account and if warranted determine appropriate action thereof in accordance with COA Circular 2016-005. Further, require the immediate submission of the liquidation reports and supporting documents for all cash advances already accounted for in the books as liquidated.

observations.recommendation:
1.13 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
a. Acting Municipal Accountant to prepare monthly bank reconciliation
statements for all five (5) bank accounts supported with bank statements and
schedules, and further to record all the reconciling items/adjustments to the
accounts accordingly, and submit subsidiary and general ledgers for CIB-LCCA for all
funds to substantiate the reported account in the consolidated financial
statements.
b. Municipal Treasurer to have a regular reconciliation with the records of the
Acting Municipal Accountant, monitor cancelled, outstanding, unreleased and
stale checks, in the recording of collection and deposits and disbursements and
other reconciling items so that appropriate adjustments could be made on a
monthly basis.

2.18 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the following responsible
officials:
A. Municipal Treasurer to:
a. Submit regularly the monthly RCDs, monthly/CRAAFs With and Without
Money Value for all funds to support the submitted issued and cancelled ORs,
reported collections and deposit slips to the Acting Municipal Accountant and
to the Audit Team and to monitor the correct ORs requisitioned, issued and
balance on hand.
b. Remit/transfer regularly SEF collection to the Cash in Local Treasury account
of the SEF.
B. Acting Municipal Accountant to:
c. Have a regular reconciliation between Collectors’, Municipal Treasurer and
the Acting Municipal Accountant’s records in the recording of collections and
deposits and other reconciling items so that appropriate adjustments could be
made on a monthly basis and to substantiate the reported Cash in Local
Treasury account in the financial statement.
d. Prepare the necessary adjusting entries for any identified reconciling items so
that the reported Cash in Local Treasury account balance of ₱286,224.62 at
year end will be corrected.

3.22 We recommended that the Acting Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting entries
for the completed and turned-over projects and record depreciation expense of the
related Property, Plant and Equipment as provided in the above cited regulations.
We further recommended that a Lapsing Schedule for Depreciation be prepared to
serve as a guide in the computation of the annual depreciation.

3.23 Also, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive create an Inventory Committee
that will lead the conduct of physical inventory of all properties owned by the
municipality and prepare and submit RPCPPE not later than January 31 of each
year.

4.5 We recommended that the Acting Municipal Accountant complies with the required
presentation and disclosure format of PPE in the Notes to FS as prescribed in Annex
G of the COA Circular 2016-004.

5.9 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive mandate the Acting Municipal
Accountant to:
a. Ensure that only those fully supported claims shall be recognized as valid
obligations with the documents supporting thereto attached to the journal
vouchers for recording in the books of accounts;
b. Ensure that classification of accounts is in accordance with the Revised Chart of
Accounts for LGUs;
c. Effect appropriate adjusting entries on the conditions observed; and
d. Maintain complete and detailed subsidiary ledgers for Accounts Payable, with
period aging schedule to aid in the monitoring of the account.

6.6 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
a. Acting Municipal Accountant to: (i) coordinate with the designated property
officer to reconstruct and reconcile their records, taking into consideration the
accuracy of the beginning inventory balances, the purchases during the year
that were recorded as inventory, the purchases during the year that were
reclassified to expenses, the actual issuances, and other related adjustments,
and prepare the necessary adjusting entries; and (ii) coordinate with the
Inventory Committee and the designated property officer to obtain the
balance of inventory per physical count at the end of the year, reconcile
balances and prepare adjusting entries, if warranted.
b. Municipal Treasurer to: (i) prepare regularly the RIS and SSMI and furnish
the Acting Municipal Accountant a copy thereof for recording; and (ii)
reconcile property records with the result of annual physical count and
accounting records.
c. Inventory Committee to: (i) submit CY 2021 RPCI; and (ii) regularly conduct
physical count of inventories and submit a copy of the RPCI to the Audit Team
not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second
semester, respectively.

7.7 We recommended the Local Chief Executive direct the Acting Municipal Accountant
to:
a. Disregard previous accounting practice and to classify transactions in accordance
with the Revised Chart of Accounts.
b. Reconcile the account balances in the financial statements and notes to financial
statements in order to present fairly the accounts and not to mislead its users.
c. Prepare correcting entries, if necessary.

8.6 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) undertake measures that
would address the causes cited by the Acting Municipal Accountant, if warranted, in
order to submit the FS on the date prescribed by the pertinent regulation in force.
We also recommended that the LCE enjoin the Acting Municipal Accountant to keep
abreast of the reports and documents, among others SCBAA, which are required to
be prepared and made available to its users on or before its prescribed dates.

9.8 We recommended that the Head of Procuring Entity to:
a. Ensure that all POs/contracts entered into by the Municipal Government
incorporates definite terms and conditions of the procured items; and
b. Establish an internal control mechanism that would ensure that all POs/contracts
entered into by Management are duly accomplished and include all the basic
information as prescribed in COA Circular No. 96-010 to protect the interest of
the Municipal Government.

10.10 We recommended that the Management strictly observe prudence on the use of
government funds with regard to the provisions of meals and snacks, and any other
transactions, by ensuring that these are utilized only for expenses which are regular
and necessary.

11.9 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
a. Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer and the Acting
Municipal Accountant to reconcile the amount of unexpended balance of the
LDRRMF between the Utilization Report and the Trust Liability-DRRMF
account reflected in the Financial Statement.
b. Acting Municipal Accountant, upon reconciliation of the unexpended balances to
draw a Journal Entry Voucher to adjust the recorded balance of Trust Liability-
DRRMF account under Trust Fund;
c. Acting Municipal Accountant to transfer the Trust Liability-DRRMF from the
General Fund to the Trust Fund; and
d. Acting Municipal Accountant to disclose the amount and details of the
unexpended balances of the LDRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statements as
required in Section 5.1.12 and 5.1.16 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

12.7 We recommended that the Acting Municipal Accountant make correcting entries to
adjust the PPE, Inventory and Government Equity accounts of the LGU to reflect the
above-mentioned findings.

13.8 We recommended the management to stop the practice of disbursing funds without
an approved appropriation as provided under the above provision in order to
ascertain the legality and propriety of disbursements.

15.6 We recommended that the LCE direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to prepare
and maintain periodically the subsidiary ledger using the correct format and on a per
grant basis for the Due to NGAs account, to be utilized as a monitoring tool for the
procurement activities, settlement of claims in the implementation of projects, and
refund of excess funds to the Source Agency after full implementation of the project.

We further recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare a corrected Bank Reconciliation Statement and immediately prepare the necessary journal entries to adjust the balance of CIB-LCCA.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to reclassify the items recorded as equipment instead of inventories and to its proper PPE account classification, as suggested in the attached proposed adjusting journal entries.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to take up the recognition of the property, plant and equipment and recognize the depreciation of the related PPEs for the current year as provided in the above cited regulations. We further recommended that a Lapsing Schedule for Depreciation be prepared to serve as a guide in the computation of annual depreciation.
observations.reason: Lapsing schedule is yet to be prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned accountable officer to submit to COA the required documents for the request for relief from accountability in compliance to the above cited regulation. We also recommended that for the meantime, the Municipal Accountant recognize accountability of the subject properties until a relief of accountability to accountable officer is granted by COA.
We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entries for the proper recognition of the income, expense and asset account in the books of the LGU.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause the maximum utilization of the LDRRM funds to help mitigate the effects of disasters that impact the LGU. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant transfer the current and previous year’s unutilized amount of the LDRRMF to the Trust Liability- DRRM account.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause the maximum utilization of the 20% economic development fund to help in the attainment of the desired socio-economic development goals of the LGU.

We recommended the GAD Focal Point System to: a) institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation; b) undertake gender analysis to identify existing gender issues and ensure that the programs, projects and activities are responsive to the said issues, and c) revisit Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 as amended by JMC 2016-01, which identifies the data requirements for particular gender issues. We further recommended that the Local Planning and Development Office spearheads the setting up and maintenance of GAD Database by coordinating with DILG Field Operation’s Officer and other concerned LGU officials.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to record the identified book reconciling items in the books of the accounts of the LGU and to account for the unreconciled amount as reported in the bank reconciliation statement under the General Fund.
observations.reason:
The unreconciled balance was still observed to be existing and the reconciling items were not yet recorded.

Reiterated in this Report

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the MPDC, Municipal Assessor, Municipal Engineer, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to coordinate in identification, inventory, measurement, recording and maintenance of records and reports for local roads and road components under the jurisdiction of the LGU.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant to fully disclose the details of the Local Road Network in the Notes to the Financial Statements and prepare adjustments to the account to reflect corrections for transaction in CY 2019.
observations.reason:
There still no Ledger Card and Property Card maintained for each local road network.  




The Road Network account was not properly disclosed in the financial statement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to observe strictly the submission of monthly trial balances and interim financial statements within the period set by regulation.
observations.managementAction: The LGU pointed out that the late submission is due to the delay in submission of reports from the MT office to the Accounting Office which are needed in the preparation of the Trial Balances.
observations.reason: Trial balances were only submitted by year-end.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Local DRRM Officer, the BAC and other concerned officers to accelerate the procurement and implementation of the needed disaster equipment and projects to improve the capacity of the Municipality to respond to emergencies and disasters. 

We also recommended that that the Municipal Accountant transfer the unutilized amount from the LDRRMF to the Trust Liability- DRRM account.
observations.reason:
There was still unutilized disaster fund for mitigation and preparedness response as of end of CY 2020. This is  discussed in Part II of this Report

The LGU has not yet transferred the unutilized portion of the LDRRMF to Trust Liability-DRRM

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Local Planning and Development Office spearheads the setting up and maintenance of GAD Database by coordinating with DILG Field Operation’s Officer and other concerned LGU officials. 

Further, we recommended the GAD Focal Point System to: a) institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation; b) undertake gender analysis to identify and confirm existing gender issues and ensure that the programs, projects and activities are responsive to the said issues, and c) revisit Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 as amended by JMC 2016-01, which identifies the data requirements for particular gender issues.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to gather all necessary data pertaining to the account and determine appropriate action thereof in accordance with COA Circular 2016-005. 

Further, require the immediate submission of the liquidation reports and supporting documents for all cash advances already accounted for in the books as liquidated.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant submitted death certificate and request letter.

observations.recommendation: Insurable Properties of the LGU are not insured with the GSIS in non-compliance to Republic Act 656 and  Proclamation No. 1081 dated September 21, 1972 and General Order No. 1, dated September 22, 1972,  thus exposing the government properties to risk of loss due to theft, fire, earthquakes, storms and other catastrophic events.
observations.reason: The management inquired at GSIS the documentary requirements needed for the insurance of its properties

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to exert effort in tracing the unreconciled balance found in the CIB-LCCA of the General Fund.
We further recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare a corrected Bank Reconciliation Statement and immediately prepare the necessary journal entries to adjust the balance of CIB-LCCA.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal General Service Officer to conduct physical inventory of all remaining items of goods, supplies, materials including drugs and medicines and medical supplies and equipment in the custody of responsible officials and determine undistributed/unconsumed items. Likewise, required the submission of Stock Cards, Requisition Issue Slip, Inventory Custodian Slip, and such other records/reports supporting issuance/distribution of the inventories.
Further, required the Municipal Accountant to recognize in the books any undistributed/unissued inventory items by preparing a Journal Entry Voucher to record the inventory items in the corresponding inventory ledger card.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the adjusting entries to reclassify the items recorded as equipment instead of inventories and to its proper PPE account classification, as suggested in the attached proposed adjusting journal entries.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to take up the recognition of the property, plant and equipment and recognize the depreciation of the related PPEs for the current year as provided in the above cited regulations. We further recommended that a Lapsing Schedule for Depreciation be prepared to serve as a guide in the computation of annual depreciation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned accountable officer to submit to COA the required documents for the request for relief from property accountability in compliance to the above cited regulation.  We also recommended that for the meantime, the Municipal Accountant recognize accountability of the subject properties until a relief of accountability to accountable officer is granted by COA.
We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entries for the proper recognition of the income, expense and asset account in the books of the LGU.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause the maximum utilization of the LDRRM funds to help mitigate the effects of disasters that impact the LGU. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant transfer the current and previous year’s unutilized amount of the LDRRMF to the Trust Liability- DRRM account.

We recommended that all programs, projects and activities pertaining to the additional disaster funds of the Municipality be incorporated in the LDRRM Plan and Budget. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant disclose all LDRRM funds in the Notes to the Financial Statements, including realigned local funds, additional funds received from the National Government, other LGUs and other agencies.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause the maximum utilization of the 20% economic development fund to help in the attainment of the desired socio-economic development goals of the LGU.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the early procurement of the projects for the optimal utilization and to enhance implementation of the PPAs under the 20% Development Fund.

We further recommended that the LCE, through the local development council identify priority development projects that will contribute to the attainment of the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the LGU.
observations.reason:
The LGU was not able to optimally utilized the 20% development fund as discussed in Part II of this report.

The LGU was able to identify PPAs for the development program of the LGU through the Local Development Council

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Human Resource Officer to revisit its guidelines in the hiring and management of Job Order/Casual personnel, maintain and regularly update the records of LGU personnel together with their contract of service, DTRs and Accomplishment Reports.
observations.reason: The LGU has already prepared and submitted Guidelines in Hiring Job Order/Casual Personnel and DTRs and Accomplishment Reports of the Job Order/ Casual personnel

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer, being the General Service Officer, to conduct and submit Report on the Physical Count on PPE and maintain property cards for each PPE account and the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPLEC), and require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records to come up with a correct balance of PPE.

We further recommend that the Municipal Accountant provide for depreciation of the Water Supply System and Other Land Improvements and present a schedule of PPE showing addition, deletion, and other changes, the related movement in the accumulated depreciation, and the book value including other required information related to the PPE account in the Notes to Financial Statement in accordance with PPSAS 17.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer partially complied by submitting the Physical count of movable PPEs only. No submission on physical count of infrastructure assets which is majority of the PPE in terms of cost.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE through the Local School Board should ensure that there is an Approved Budget for the Special Education Fund so that priority projects for the fund shall be implemented. Further, the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer should cause the recognition of the correct amount of Special Education Tax and transfer the same to SEF account regularly.
observations.reason: The LGU formulated and submitted CY 2020 budget for SEF

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LCE through the Local School Board should ensure that there is an Approved Budget for the Special Education Fund so that priority projects for the fund shall be implemented. Further, the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer should cause the recognition of the correct amount of Special Education Tax and transfer the same to SEF account regularly.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to prepare a monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for all the bank
accounts of the agency to achieve an accurate financial reporting of Cash in Bank
account.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Treasurer, being the General Service Officer, to conduct and submit Report on
the Physical Count on PPE and maintain property cards for each PPE account
and the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update Property, Plant and
Equipment Ledger Cards (PPLEC), and require the Municipal Treasurer and the
Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records to come up with a correct
balance of PPE.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide for
depreciation of the Water Supply System and Other Land Improvements and
present a schedule of PPE showing addition, deletion, and other changes, the
related movement in the accumulated depreciation, and the book value including
other required information related to the PPE account in the Notes to Financial
Statement in accordance with PPSAS 17.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to observe strictly the submission of monthly trial balances and
interim financial statements within the period set by regulation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Planning and Development Officer and other concerned officials to speed up the
implementation and strict monitoring of development projects to ensure efficient
and effective utilization of the said funds which will contribute to the social and
economic development as well as environment protection of the constituents.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive in coordination with the
Local DRRM Officer and other concerned officers to accelerate the procurement
and implementation of the needed disaster equipment and projects to improve the
capacity of the Municipality to respond to emergencies and disasters.

We recommended that the Solid Waste Management Board headed by the
Local Chief Executive to speed up the acquisition of necessary facilities for the
controlled dump site and other plans incorporated in the LGUs 10 year Ecological
Waste Management Plan. We recommend further to make ordinances in relation to
solid waste management fees to support its operation and achieve the
objectives/purpose of the Ecological Solid Waste Management Program.

We recommended that the LGU review the hiring procedure and management
of Job Order/Casual personnel and establish a policy and guideline in accordance
with Articles 163 and 164 of the IRR, RA 7160 and SCS Resolution No. 020790. Also,
require the immediate submission of the Accomplishment Reports and Daily Time
Records for all payments made to JO/Casual personnel in accordance with COA
Circular No. 2012-001. This is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of
Suspension and/or Disallowance, if warranted.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the GAD Focal Point
System to : a) institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically
gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the basis for gender analysis,
planning, programming and policy formulation; b) undertake gender analysis to
identify and confirm existing gender issues and insure that the programs, projects
and activities are responsive to the said issues, and c) revisit Annex B of PCW-DILG-
DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01, which identifies the data
requirements for particular gender issues.

We recommended further that the GAD Accomplishment Report should
provide a breakdown of the expenditures for all GAD activities to facilitate review of
the fund.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare immediately the Bank Reconciliation Statements for all bank accounts of the agency to achieve an accurate financial reporting of Cash in Bank account.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant
observations.reason: Delayed issuance of the Bank Reconciliation Statements for all bank accounts of the municipality

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to inquire and secure the required procedure for the write-off of account settlement/collectability thereof in accordance with COA Circular 2016-005. Further, require the immediate submission of the liquidation reports and supporting documents for all cash advances already accounted for in the books as liquidated.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant to gather all necessary data pertaining to the account and determine appropriate action thereof in accordance with COA Circular 2016-005. Further, require the immediate submission of the liquidation reports and supporting documents for all cash advances already accounted for in the books as liquidated.
observations.reason: Submitted to COA Death Certificate of Paul Ramas.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to present a Schedule of property, plant and equipment showing the addition, deletion and other changes, the related movement in the accumulated depreciation, and the book value including other required information related to the PPE account in the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with PPSAS 17. We further recommend that the General Services Office or the Municipal Treasurer conduct a complete physical inventory of its PPE, maintain and update Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) and Property Cards, and require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records to come up with a correct balance of PPE.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant to present a Schedule of property, plant and equipment and the General Services Officer or the Municipal Treasurer to conduct a complete physical inventory and maintain property cards and PPELC and reconcile records.
observations.reason: Difficulty in determining the cost of some infrastructure properties that have been acquired during the previous administrations; lack of personnel and time to conduct complete physical inventory of its PPE.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly comply with the requirements in the preparation of the Requests for Quotation and Purchase Order as provided for under RA 9184 and COA Circular No. 96-010.
observations.managementAction: BAC as responsible office for the preparation of RFQ and PO.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant and the BAC Chairman to immediately submit the disbursement vouchers and supporting documents and direct the person(s) involved to strictly adhere to the timely compliance of submission of the required documents.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Treasurer and Accountant and BAC Chairman to immediately submit the disbursement vouchers and supporting documents and direct the person(s) involved to strictly adhere to the timely compliance of submission of the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Officials concerned in formulating its local development plan and Annual Investment Plan to consider the allowable development projects as identified under DILG/DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 and be guided by the plan in the utilization of the fund, whenever possible, and refrain from charging expenditures not allowed by law to attain the desired socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: The LGU already considered some of the PPAs which do not include the nature of capital expenditure when formulating its local development plan and Annual Investment Plan. The LGU will be guided by the plan in the utilization of the fund.
observations.reason: Some PPAs were included which do not partake the nature of capital expenditure or are not allowed but the LGU will be guided by the plan and realign where necessary.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to transfer the unexpended/unobligated Mitigation Fund specifically the dormant MOOE amounting P122,951.51 to the Trust Fund and to make the necessary adjusting entry as provided under COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant to transfer the unutilized/unobligated Mitigation Fund amounting P122,951.51 to the Special Trust Fund account and to make the necessary adjusting entry as provided under COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.reason: The Fund was already provided for transfer; the LGU will request activation of the account.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to implement the Ecological Solid Waste Management Program of the LGU without delay in order to achieve the objectives/purposes of the program.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to implement the Ecological Solid Waste Management Program of the LGU without delay.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LCE through the Local School Board should ensure that there is an Approved Budget for the Special Education Fund so that priority projects for the fund shall be implemented. Further, the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer should cause the recognition of the correct amount of Special Education Tax and transfer the same to SEF account regularly.
observations.managementAction: Local School Board to ensure that there is an Approved Budget for the Special Education Fund and Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to recognize the correct amount of Special Education Tax and transfer the same to SEF account regularly.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LGU procure the services of an appraiser to prepare a list of insurable assets of the LGU and thereafter insure the insurable properties of the LGU to minimize risk of loss.
observations.managementAction: LGU to procure the services of an appraiser to prepare a list of insurable assets of the LGU and thereafter insure the insurable properties.
observations.reason: The LGU procured insurance for some vehicles but other properties were not yet insured; appraiser services to be engaged.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LCE shall ensure that the SEF are managed, utilized, and expended for its purpose as mandated by the Law.
observations.reason: No Approved Budget for 2017

observations.recommendation: Insurable Properties of the LGU should be insured with the GSIS in accordance with RA 656, Proclamation No. 1081 and General Order No. 1.
observations.reason: No physical Inventory of PPE was conducted for motor vehicles and infrastructures

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the Bank Reconciliation Statements for all bank accounts with complete supporting documents such as Bank Statements, paid checks and other related documents, so that all reconciling items can be properly/accurately accounted for. Henceforth the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements should be submitted within the reglementary period for verification purposes, pursuant to Section 74 of P.D. 1445 and Sec. 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to gather all necessary data pertaining to the account and determine appropriate action thereof in accordance with COA Circular 2016-005. Further, require the immediate submission of the liquidation reports and supporting documents for all cash advances already accounted for in the books as liquidated.
This observation is without prejudice to the issuances of Notice of Suspension and/or Disallowance, if warranted.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to present a Schedule of property, plant and equipment showing the addition, deletion, and other changes, the related movement in the accumulated depreciation, and the book value including other required information related to the PPE account in the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with PPSAS 17.

We further recommend to (a) conduct a complete physical count of all properties of the Municipality and submit a complete report on the physical count, using the prescribed format, (b) maintain and update Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) and Property Cards, and (c) require the General Services Officer or the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records to come up with a correct balance of PPE.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly comply with the requirements in the preparation of the Requests for Quotation and Purchase Order as provided for under RA 9184 and COA Circular No. 96-010.

This observation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension and/or Disallowance, if warranted.

We recommend the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant and the BAC Chairman to immediately submit the disbursement vouchers and contracts together with their required supporting documents and direct the person(s) involved to strictly adhere to the timely compliance of submission of the required documents.

This observation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension and/or Disallowance, if warranted.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Officials concerned that in formulating its local development plan and Annual Investment Plan to consider the allowable development projects as identified under DILG/DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 and be guided by the plan in the utilization of the fund, whenever possible, and refrain from charging expenditures not allowed by law to attain the desired socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes of the LGU.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to transfer the unutilized/unobligated Mitigation Fund amounting P122,951.51 to the Special Trust Fund Account and to make the necessary adjusting entry as provided under COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to implement the Ecological Solid Waste Management Program of the LGU without delay in order to achieve the objectives/purposes of the program.

We recommend that the LCE through the Local School Board should ensure that there is an Approved Budget for the Special Education Fund so that priority projects for the fund shall be implemented. Further, the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer should cause the recognition of the correct amount of Special Education Tax and transfer the same to SEF account regularly.
We recommend that Management should adhere strictly to the provision of JMC No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 -001 The management did not incur operating expenses charged to the fund
observations.reason: Implement the fund includes PPAs which do not partake the nature of capital expenditures

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LGU prepare a Solid Waste Management Plan in accordance with RA 9003, formalize a change management strategy in the implementation of a realistic and doable Solid Waste Management Plan with the application of the SMART strategy, develop an Action/Implementation Plan, Draw a robust governance arrangement for the implementation, monitoring and measurement of results, and to establish accountability for results.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 -002 The management submitted a 10-year SWMP (2017-2026)
observations.reason: Submitted a 10-year SWMP (2017-2026)

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management direct the GAD Focal Person to prepare a GAD Plan in accordance with related guidelines, allocate at least five percent of the total agency appropriations, and periodic/ complete reporting of GAD Activities.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 -004 The GAD Focal Person already prepared a plan in accordance with the related guidelines.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the Municipal General Services Officer to: (a) submit the Report of the Physical Inventory of the Property, Plant and Equipment every year as mandated by law, and to maintain and update the property cards and reconcile the records with the Accountant and, (b) to create an inventory committee who will conduct the annual physical inventory of PPE as mandated by Section 156 of COA Circular 92-386.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 -005 Partially implemented; submitted a partial Report of physical count of office equipment and semi-expendable properties
observations.reason: Partially submitted a partial Report of physical count of office equipment and semi-expendable properties

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LCE should strengthen the coordination among stakeholders by fully adhering to the Memorandum of Agreement on the grants from NGAs and the related laws, rules and regulations to ensure that the project are properly implemented. Moreover, for transparency purposes, there should be a tool to properly monitor the project from its inception to its completion.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 -007 The management already submitted the required documents.
observations.reason: Submitted the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive directs the person(s) involved to strictly adhere to the timely compliance of submission of the documents enumerated in the preceding paragraph pursuant to COA Circular 2009-001 and RA 9184. Moreover, as a control of the LGU, we enjoin the LCE to desist from approving the payment of contractual obligations without first ensuring that the contract documents have been properly prepared and received by the Audit team.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 -008 The management already submitted the required documents.
observations.reason: Submitted the required documents.

observations.recommendation: Immediately submit the lacking supporting documents. This is without prejudice to any disallowances that may be issued, Stop the practice of paying without complete supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit all the lacking disbursement vouchers and supporting documents, as well as the cancelled checks. This is without prejudice to any notices of suspension, charges or disallowances that may be issued as a result of our audit.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 -010 The management already submitted the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor directs the MDRRMO to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant on the timely submission of the Monthly Reports on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the Audit Team using the format prescribed in Annex B of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and other related guidelines. While the Municipal Accountant should record all related transactions completely, properly classify related accounts, and fully disclose the LDRRMF in accordance with the stated accounting guidelines.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 -011 The LDRMMO submitted the monthly report on DRRM fund utilization.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant should record all related transactions completely, properly classify related accounts, and fully disclose the LDRRMF in accordance with the stated accounting guidelines.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LCE directs the person(s) concerned to prepare a Physical and Financial Plan for the IF/CF and implement the related programs, projects and activities only in accordance with the Plan in compliance with JC 2015-01, unless otherwise provided by law, and submit the liquidation supporting documents to ICFAU to avoid issuance of the Notice of Suspension for such cash advances.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 -012 The management submitted the required documents.
observations.reason: Submitted the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive supervise the Disbursing Officer and the Municipal Accountant in the conduct of their duty to ensure that all cash advances are liquidated within the reglementary period as soon as the purpose for which it was given has been served in compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 17, 1997 and Section 100 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 -013 The management is exerting efforts for dropping of the accounts. Request for dropping of account was submitted to the COA Team.
observations.reason: Request for dropping of account was submitted to the COA Team

observations.recommendation: A successful revenue collection is the “lifeblood” of every local government unit, as it is through here that they are provided with adequate resources to discharge their functions and effectively carry out planned projects, programs and activities. We recommend: • Review the local tax code. • Create a system to enhance the collections of the local revenues. • Direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor to update their records to create an efficient collection of the Revenues. • Conduct frequent physical surveys to ensure that all real properties are properly listed, regularly appraised and actually paid on time.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 -014 Updated Local Revenue Code already submitted

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant and all concerned official to submit immediately all lacking documents. Management is encouraged to exercise prudence on the use of their resources and maximize its utilization for more worthwhile projects.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management require the Municipal General Services Officer
to: (a) submit the Report of the Physical Inventory of the Property, Plant and
Equipment every year as mandated by law, and to maintain and update the
property cards and reconcile the records with the Accountant and, (b) to create an
inventory committee who will conduct the annual physical inventory of PPE as
mandated by Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386.

We recommend that the LCE should require the GSO to insure all Insurable
Properties of the LGU the soonest possible time to ensure that the LGU will be
indemnified in case of losses due to manmade and catastrophic events.

We recommend that the LCE should strengthen the coordination among
stakeholders by fully adhering to the Memorandum of Agreement on the grants
from NGAs and the related laws, rules and regulations to ensure that the project are
properly implemented. Moreover, for transparency purposes, there should be a tool
to properly monitor the project from its inception to its completion.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive directs the person(s) involved to
strictly adhere to the timely compliance of submission of the documents enumerated
in the preceding paragraph pursuant to COA Circular 2009-001 and RA 9184.
Moreover, as a control of the LGU, we enjoin the LCE to desist from approving the
payment of contractual obligations without first ensuring that the contract
documents have been properly prepared and received by the Audit Team.

We recommend and Management accepted to adhere strictly to the provision
of JMC JMC No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.

We recommend that the LGU prepare a Solid Waste Management Plan in
accordance in accordance with RA 9003, formalize a change management strategy
in the implementation of a realistic and doable Solid Waste Management Plan with
the application of the SMART strategy, develop an Action/Implementation Plan,
Draw a robust governance arrangement for the Plan and Identify possible risks in a
comprehensive manner for SWMB to facilitate proper implementation, monitoring
and measurement of results, and to establish accountability for results.

We recommend that the LCE shall ensure that the SEF are managed, utilized
and expended for its purpose as mandated by the Law.

We recommend that Management direct the GAD Focal Person to prepare a
GAD Plan in accordance with related guidelines, allocate at least five percent of the
total agency appropriations, and to ensure full utilization of the budget allocated and
periodic/ complete reporting of GAD Activities.

We recommend that the MDRRMO will coordinate with the Municipal
Accountant on the timely submission of the Monthly Reports on Sources and
Utilization of DRRMF to the Audit Team using the format prescribed in Annex B of
COA Circular No. 2012-002 and other related prescribed guidelines. While the
Municipal Accountant should record all related transactions completely, properly
classify related accounts, and fully disclose the LDRRMF in accordance with the
stated accounting guidelines.

We recommend that the LCE directs the person(s) concerned to prepare a Physical
and Financial Plan for the IF/CF and implement the related programs, projects and
activities only in accordance with the Plan in compliance to JC 2015-01, unless
otherwise provided by law, and submit the liquidation supporting documents to
ICFAU to avoid issuance of the Notice of Suspension for such cash advances.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive should supervise the Disbursing
Officer and the Municipal Accountant in the conduct of their duty to ensure that all
cash advances are liquidated within the reglementary period as soon as the purpose
for which it was given has been served in compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002
dated February 17, 1997 and Section 100 of PD 1445.

Immediately submit the lacking supporting documents. This is without
prejudice to any disallowances that may be issued, of warranted. Stop the practice

We recommend the AAR 2015- municipal accountant reconcile immediately the reciprocal accounts under the three funds by looking into the records and documents affecting the said account. Verify the causes of the difference and prepare the necessary adjusting/correcting entries, then submit the Journal Entry Voucher with complete supporting documents to the Audit Team for verification purposes. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant should always analyze and record all transaction at once, upon its occurrence, specially for transactions with reciprocal accounts to avoid recurrence of observation. Ensure that the accounting data/reports produced should be timely, accurate, reliable and meaningful in relation to an identified need. All recorded data should portray accurately the financial transactions and results, in a manner that will avoid release of misleading reports.
observations.managementAction: already reconciled

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit all the lacking checks issued, totaling P2,196,545.30 and submit all the Disbursement Vouchers with supporting documents to the Audit Team for verification purposes. Henceforth, all disbursement vouchers/supporting documents and copies of the checks be submitted on a regular and timely basis.
observations.managementAction: submitted all the lacking checks issued, totaling P2,196,545.30

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LGU should exert extra efforts in the acquisition of lots for the said projects. The management already realigned the said project to the other development projects. If it would be impossible for them to acquire the said lots, then they should consider the other development projects which are beneficial to the community, and still chargeable to the 20% Development Fund, and submit them to Sanggunian for realignment purposes.
observations.managementAction: management realigned the said project to the other development projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LGUs’ MSWD Focal Person should strictly implement the terms and condition of the MOA on Supplemental/Feeding Program entered into with the DSWD. Ensure that the duties and functions of concerned officials are effectively discharged to achieve the desired program objectives to provide improved and sustainable nutritional status of the target children beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: management submitted the said lacking documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management should enforce the ceiling of P1,200/day for seminar, conventions, etc. sponsored by non-government and private organizations to prevent disallowances in future transactions.

Management should strictly submit all perfected Contracts & Purchase Orders together with its supporting documents to the Audit Team within five working days from perfection thereof as required per COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the contracts/PO & promise to submit the said documents on time.

observations.recommendation: Ensure that all Fund Transfers from the National Government Agencies is supported with a Memorandum of Agreement for the Audit Team to verify the purpose and propriety of the said transfer and to provide details of the program/projects to be undertaken, and the related requirements for liquidation. Immediately liquidate the fund transfer within the time frame, as soon as the purpose is accomplished.
observations.managementAction: management promised to submit the said MOA to the Audit Team.
